summary: I was born in the village of Muang Cha on June 15th, 1950. This place is very dear to me because it is where my family lived and where many important events happened, including the crash of an American plane near our farmland. Muang Cha is surrounded by jungle, mountains, and farmland, which played a big role in how we helped the stranded pilot during the war.

My husband is Colonel Shong Leng Xiong, and he was a soldier during the war. He came to our camp to train, and later we got married. Together, we fought in the war, and I remember when we helped a pilot who was shot down in the jungle; my husband reassured him by showing our American radio. Colonel Shong Leng Xiong was not only my husband but also my partner in the struggles we faced during those difficult times.

The United States played a significant role in my story because the pilot we helped was American. My father and I rescued him after his plane crashed near our farmland. Later, the pilot returned to thank us and even brought a Thanksgiving meal to share. The American soldiers and their radios were a symbol of hope and friendship amidst the war.

Thailand is where one of the American pilots I knew died. Although he was a great friend and had come back to thank us for saving him, he did not survive in Thailand. His death was a reminder of the many losses we endured during the war, and it made me cherish the memories of those who lived and those who did not.

Laos is the country where I lived and where much of the war took place. It was also where another pilot died after being shot down. My family and I faced many dangers here, including attacks from communists. Laos is the backdrop of my story, filled with both hardship and resilience.

Thanksgiving became a special day for me because the American pilot I helped brought us two turkeys to celebrate it. He said that if we hadn't saved him, he wouldn't be able to celebrate Thanksgiving that year. Sharing that meal with him was a moment of gratitude and friendship amidst the war, and it remains one of my greatest memories.
